10. Attachment styles in cats.
Recent studies show that cats, much like
children, can develop attachment styles:
secure, anxious, or avoidant. These
labels, often used in human psychology,
also apply to how cats relate to their
caregivers.
A securely attached cat will explore
confidently, return to you for comfort,
and show resilience in stressful
situations. An insecure cat might hide,
vocalize excessively, or react strongly
to your absence.
